{"step_by_step_thinking": "The documents indicate that at low infusion rates (0.5 to 2 mcg/kg per minute), dopamine acts on the visceral vasculature to produce vasodilation, including the kidneys, resulting in increased urinary flow. However, the query specifies 3-5 mcg/kg/min, which falls into the intermediate infusion rates. Intermediate infusion rates (from 2 to 10 mcg/kg/min) stimulate myocardial contractility and increase electrical conductivity in the heart leading to increased cardiac output. Therefore, at 3-5 mcg/kg/min, dopamine produces increased cardiac contractility.", "answer_choice": "C"}
